sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Right knee pain
History of Present Illness
James reports right knee pain for the past few days, exacerbated by activity, particularly cycling. He describes the pain as increasing in severity over the last three or four days. He notes some swelling and mild erythema in the knees, particularly in the mornings. He denies any injury or trauma. He has a history of coronary artery disease and lupus, currently managed with aspirin and Crestor. He was off Plavix until recently. He reports no chest pain or shortness of breath.
Findings
Physical exam reveals bilateral knee edema and mild erythema. Range of motion is decreased bilaterally. A slight effusion is noted on the right knee. A grade 2/6 systolic ejection murmur is auscultated. The patient's lungs are clear to auscultation. The patient's heart rhythm is regular. The EKG shows no acute abnormalities. The vital signs are stable.
Assessment
The patient's right knee pain is concerning for a possible flare of his underlying lupus or secondary to overuse. His coronary artery disease is stable.
Plan
Order an autoimmune panel to evaluate for lupus flare. Order a lipid panel and echocardiogram to assess his coronary artery disease. Schedule a follow-up appointment in one week to assess response to treatment and review lab results.
